hero.imageAlt: Aerial view of island buildings surrounded by forest and fjord
hero.title: Sleeping & Living
hero.subtitle: Intentionally simple, shared, and close to nature.

children.text: The island has two houses — a main house and a smaller annex — with space for up to 20 people in total. The accommodation is comfortable and functional, designed for groups who want to cook together, spend time outdoors, and sleep well.

buildingImages.alt: Aerial view of both island buildings
Main house exterior with stairs and pine tree

sleepingSection.heading: Sleeping

children.text: The main house has six bedrooms with a mix of single and bunk beds. The annex has two additional rooms. All beds are made up with fresh linen and blankets.
There are four bathrooms across the two buildings, each with hot showers. The island has its own freshwater source.

sleepingSection.imageAlt: Main house with deck, garden and dog

children.text: The main living and dining area is a large open room with windows facing the fjord. There is a fully equipped kitchen, a long dining table for communal meals, and a fireplace for evenings.
There is also a small reading room, a terrace with outdoor furniture, and a jetty for boats and swimming.

galleryImages.alt: Gathering room with tables and sunflower paintings
Large dining room with wooden floors
Living room with fireplace and couches
People cooking together in the kitchen
Outdoor picnic tables with fjord view
Yellow house exterior with wooden deck

children.text: For larger gatherings, the island has an outdoor fire circle with seating for 25–30 people, and a covered outdoor space suitable for workshops, ceremonies, or shared meals in most weather.

closingImage.alt: Outdoor volleyball net surrounded by forest
